zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas de Burgh , Lord of Borough Green於1268年出生在英國坎布利其爾斯托福 姆 吉時，他的父親John de Burgh32歲，母親Cecily de Balliol31歲。他於1299年與Lucy Bellew在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德利丁米爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1315年在英國越可爾哈福斯享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas de Burgh , Lord of Borough Green于1268年出生在英国坎布利其爾斯托福 姆 吉时，他的父亲John de Burgh32岁，母亲Cecily de Balliol31岁。他于1299年与Lucy Bellew在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德利丁米爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1315年在英国越可爾哈福斯享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas de Burgh , Lord of Borough Green was born in 1268, in Stow cum Quy, Cambridgeshire, England, his father, John de Burgh, was 32 and his mother, Cecily de Balliol, was 31. He married Lucy Bellew in 1299, in Ridingmill, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1315, in Hackforth, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas de Burgh , Lord of Borough Green est né en 1268, à Stow cum Quy, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Burgh, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Balliol, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Lucy Bellew en 1299, à Ridingmill, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1315, à Hackforth, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans.

ko.Stow Cum Quy, Cambridgeshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 스타우 쿠 크이
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Burgh , Lord of Borough Green(이)가 1268년 영국 캄브리지실르 스타우 쿠 크이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Burgh(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecily de Balliol(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1299년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 리딩미에서 Lucy Bellew(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1315년 영국 요르크실르 해ck포스에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Lucy Bellew em 1299, em Ridingmill, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.Stow Cum Quy, Cambridgeshire, England: стоу кум куи, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas de Burgh , Lord of Borough Green родился в 1268 г. в местности стоу кум куи, камбриджешир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Burgh было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Balliol, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy Bellew в 1299 г. в местности ридингмилл, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1315 г. в местности хаккфорт, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет.
